content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Joseph R. Syrnkowick 
Chief Counsel 
CA state Department of Education 
P.O. Box 944272 
Sacramento, 
Ca 
94244-2720 
Dear Mr. Symkowick: 
May 16, 1986 
Re: 
FPPC No. A-86-114 
This is in reply to your letter dated April 7, 1986, in 
which you asked for advice on behalf of Superintendent of public 
Instruction Bill Honig. 
You asked if Mr. Honig would incur any disclosure 
obligations under the campaign provisions of the Political Reform 
Act in connection with use of his state assigned car to travel to 
campaign events. 
You indicated that because Mr. Honig is up for 
re-election this year, he will attend campaign events at various 
times. 
As a statewide constitutional officer identified in the 
succession he uses a state assigned vehicle and is accompanied by 
a state police officer in going from campaign event to business 
and vice versa; that Mr. Honig is always carrying out his duties 
as Superintendent of Public Instruction and is in constant contact 
with management at the Department of Education. 
We have determined that, because use of the state vehicle 
and its communications equipment is necessary at all times to Mr. 
Honig's protection and performance of his official duties, Mr. 
Honig has not received a campaign contribution from the state and, 
therefore, has no obligations in connection with such use. 
Thank you for your inquiry. 
Please call me if you have any 
questions. 
JP:kt 
Sincerely, 
Jeanne Pritchard 
Division Chief, Technical 
Assistance & Analysis Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 


April 7, 1986 
Jean Pritchard, Counsel 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P.o. BOX 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
RE: 
USE OF STATE-ASSIGNED VEHICLE 
Dear Ms. Pritchard: 
I am writing this letter as a followup to our phone 
conversation of April 2, 1986. 
As you know, I am Counsel to the Department of Education 
and Bill Honig, Superintendent of Public Instruction. 
Mr. 
Honig is a statewide elected constitutional officer serving 
currently in the fourth year of his first four-year term. 
He is running for re-election at this time. 
As I explained 
to you as a constitutional officer identified in the 
succession, Mr. Honig is assigned a state unmarked vehicle 
that is usually driven by an assigned state policeman who 
accompanies him for protection. 
Mr. Honig is always 
carrying out his duties as Superintendent of Public 
Instruction and is in constant contact with management at 
the Department of Education. 
Mr. Honig is also campaigning 
and at various times throughout the day will attend 
campaign events. 
In going from campaign event to business 
and vice versa, he uses the state-assigned vehicle and is 
accompanied by the state police officer. 
Often times while 
at a campaign event, he will be contacted by telephone for 
state related business purposes. 
On behalf of Mr. Honig I have contacted the Department 
of Personnel Administration and based on that contact, it 
is my understanding that nothing further is required of 
Mr. Honig than to account for the campaign related use of 
the state vehicle. 
This accounting would be reported to 
the Internal Revenue Service in accordance with their rules 
and regulations (see attached memorandum to Joyce Whiten, 
Depa tme t of Personnel Admini tration). 
tele 
ne 
inquiry to you was for the purpose of ascertaining whether 
any further reporting or other requirements would be placed 
upon Mr. Honig's use of the state vehicle in relation to 
the campaign. 
You indicated that there had been no 


JEAN PRITCHARD 
April 7, 1986 
page 2 
Due to the oversight functions of the Fair Political 
Practices Commission regarding campaign activities, we 
would request your advice regarding what, if anything, is 
required of Mr. Honig in relation to his use of the state-
assigned vehicle for campaign purposes. 
Should you have 
any need for further information, please do not hesitate to 
contact me. 
T 
k you in advance for your assistance. 
Counse 
gal Office 
445-4694
